Component.signature: @Component(metadata: { selector?: string; standalone?: boolean; imports?: Array<Type<any>>; template?: string; templateUrl?: string; styles?: string[]; styleUrls?: string[]; changeDetection?: ChangeDetectionStrategy; encapsulation?: ViewEncapsulation; providers?: Provider[]; host?: { [key: string]: string } })
Component.description: Component metadata interface for the @Component decorator.

OnInit.description: Lifecycle hook interface called once after the first ngOnChanges.

OnDestroy.description: Lifecycle hook interface called when a directive/component is destroyed.

OnChanges.description: Lifecycle hook interface called when data-bound input properties change.

AfterViewInit.description: Lifecycle hook interface called after the component view is initialized.

AfterContentInit.description: Lifecycle hook interface called after projected content is initialized.

DoCheck.description: Lifecycle hook interface called during every change detection run.

AfterViewChecked.description: Lifecycle hook interface called after every check of the component view.

AfterContentChecked.description: Lifecycle hook interface called after every check of projected content.

PipeTransform.signature: interface PipeTransform { transform(value: any, ...args: any[]): any }
PipeTransform.description: Interface that all Angular pipes must implement.

Signal.description: A reactive value which can be read by calling it. Consumers are notified when the value changes.

WritableSignal.signature: interface WritableSignal<T> extends Signal<T> { set(value: T): void; update(updateFn: (value: T) => T): void }
WritableSignal.description: A signal whose value can be set, updated, and mutated directly.

InputSignal.description: A signal representing a component input. Read-only from the component side.

OutputEmitterRef.signature: interface OutputEmitterRef<T> { emit(value: T): void; subscribe(fn: (value: T) => void): OutputRefSubscription }
OutputEmitterRef.description: Reference returned by the output() function for emitting events.

ModelSignal.description: A writable signal used for two-way binding with the model() function.

ResourceRef.signature: interface ResourceRef<T> { value: Signal<T | undefined>; status: Signal<ResourceStatus>; isLoading: Signal<boolean>; error: Signal<unknown> }
ResourceRef.description: Reference to a resource created with the resource() function.

EffectRef.description: Reference to an effect that allows manual destruction.

FormControl.description: Tracks the value and validation status of an individual form control.

FormGroup.signature: class FormGroup<TControl extends { [K in keyof TControl]: AbstractControl<any> } = any> extends AbstractControl
FormGroup.description: Tracks the value and validity state of a group of form controls.

FormArray.signature: class FormArray<TControl extends AbstractControl<any> = any> extends AbstractControl
FormArray.description: Tracks the value and validity of an array of form controls.

AbstractControl.signature: abstract class AbstractControl<TValue = any, TRawValue extends TValue = TValue>
AbstractControl.description: Base class for FormControl, FormGroup, and FormArray. Provides shared logic for value, validity, and status.

AsyncValidatorFn.signature: interface AsyncValidatorFn { (control: AbstractControl): Promise<ValidationErrors | null> | Observable<ValidationErrors | null> }

Routes.description: An array of Route objects used to configure the Angular router.

Route.signature: interface Route { path?: string; component?: Type<any>; loadComponent?: () => Promise<Type<any>>; loadChildren?: () => Promise<Routes>; redirectTo?: string; pathMatch?: 'full' | 'prefix'; canActivate?: Array<CanActivateFn>; children?: Routes; data?: Data; resolve?: ResolveData; title?: string | Type<Resolve<string>> }
Route.description: Configuration for a single route in the Angular router.

CanActivateFn.signature: type CanActivateFn = (route: ActivatedRouteSnapshot, state: RouterStateSnapshot) => Observable<boolean | UrlTree> | Promise<boolean | UrlTree> | boolean | UrlTree
CanActivateFn.description: Functional type for route activation guards.

CanDeactivateFn.signature: type CanDeactivateFn<T> = (component: T, currentRoute: ActivatedRouteSnapshot, currentState: RouterStateSnapshot, nextState: RouterStateSnapshot) => Observable<boolean | UrlTree> | Promise<boolean | UrlTree> | boolean | UrlTree
CanDeactivateFn.description: Functional type for route deactivation guards.

ResolveFn.signature: type ResolveFn<T> = (route: ActivatedRouteSnapshot, state: RouterStateSnapshot) => Observable<T> | Promise<T> | T

HttpInterceptorFn.signature: type HttpInterceptorFn = (req: HttpRequest<unknown>, next: HttpHandlerFn) => Observable<HttpEvent<unknown>>

HttpRequest.description: An outgoing HTTP request with an optional typed body. Instances are immutable.

HttpResponse.description: A full HTTP response, including a typed body.

HttpEvent.signature: type HttpEvent<T> = HttpSentEvent | HttpHeaderResponse | HttpProgressEvent | HttpResponse<T> | HttpUserEvent<T>
HttpEvent.description: Union type for all possible HTTP event types in the response stream.

HttpErrorResponse.description: An HTTP response representing an error or failure.

ChangeDetectionStrategy.description: Defines the strategy the default change detector uses to detect changes.

ViewEncapsulation.description: Defines the CSS encapsulation strategy for component styles.

EventEmitter.signature: class EventEmitter<T = any> extends Subject<T> { emit(value?: T): void; subscribe(next?: (value: T) => void, error?: (error: any) => void, complete?: () => void): Subscription }
EventEmitter.description: An extension of RxJS Subject used to emit output events from components and directives.

Observable.signature: class Observable<T> { subscribe(observer?: Partial<Observer<T>>): Subscription; pipe(...operators: OperatorFunction<any, any>[]): Observable<any> }
Observable.description: Represents a stream of values over time. Core RxJS type used extensively in Angular.

Subject.signature: class Subject<T> extends Observable<T> implements SubscriptionLike
Subject.description: A special type of Observable that allows values to be multicast to many Observers.

BehaviorSubject.signature: class BehaviorSubject<T> extends Subject<T> { getValue(): T }
BehaviorSubject.description: A Subject variant that requires an initial value and emits its current value to new subscribers.

TemplateRef.description: Represents an embedded template used to instantiate embedded views.

ElementRef.description: Wrapper around a native element inside a View.

ViewContainerRef.description: Container for dynamically creating and managing views.

Renderer2.description: Platform-agnostic abstraction for rendering DOM elements.

Injector.signature: abstract class Injector { get<T>(token: ProviderToken<T>, notFoundValue?: T, flags?: InjectFlags): T }
Injector.description: Abstract class for dependency injection. Retrieves instances by token.

InjectionToken.signature: class InjectionToken<T> { constructor(description: string, options?: { providedIn?: Type<any> | 'root'; factory: () => T }) }
InjectionToken.description: Creates a token for dependency injection when the type is not sufficient to identify a dependency.

Provider.signature: type Provider = TypeProvider | ValueProvider | ClassProvider | ConstructorProvider | ExistingProvider | FactoryProvider | any[]
Provider.description: Union type for all supported dependency injection provider types.

EnvironmentProviders.description: Encapsulates providers that are only meant to be used in environment injector contexts.

ApplicationConfig.description: Configuration options for bootstrapping a standalone application.

DestroyRef.signature: abstract class DestroyRef { onDestroy(callback: () => void): () => void }
DestroyRef.description: Registers cleanup callbacks invoked when the associated context is destroyed.

QueryList.description: Live list maintained by Angular for @ViewChildren and @ContentChildren queries.

SimpleChanges.description: Hash of changes to input properties, keyed by property name.
